content_en: The disbelievers, whom the Messenger fought, affirmed (tawhid of Allah's Lordship) that Allah is the Creator and Disposer of all affairs, yet their affirmation did not enter them into Islam and the proof of that is the Saying of Allah, "Say, 'Who provides for you from the sky and from the earth? Or Who owns your hearing and sight? And Who brings out the living from the dead and brings out the dead from the living? And Who disposes the affairs? They will say, 'Allah.'" [surah Yunus (10): 31] - 29 - by Shaikh Salih ibn Fawzan al-Fawzan The First Foundation: The disbelievers whom the Messenger fought affirmed tawhid ar-Rububiyah (Oneness of Allah's Lordship), yet their affirmation of tawhid ar-Rububiyah did not enter them into Islam and did not sanctify their blood or wealth. This proves that tawhid is not just the affirmation of Rububiyah (Allah's Lordship), and Shirk is not just the Shirk in Rububiyah (Allah's Lordship). Rather there has never been anyone who committed Shirk in Allah's Lordship except some strange amongst the creation. Otherwise, all the nations affirmed tawhid ar-Rububiyah (Allah's Lordship). (The definition of) tawhid ar-Rububiyah (Oneness of Allah's Lordship) is affirming that Allah is the Creator, the Provider, - 30 - tawhid ar-Rububiyah is to single out Allah in His Actions. No one from the creation claimed that there is someone else who creates, provides or gives life and causes death alongside Allah. Rather, the disbelievers used to affirm that Allah is the Creator, the Provider, the One Who gives live and causes death and the Sustainer. Allah says, "If you ask them (i.e., the disbelievers), 'Who has created the heavens and the earth?' They will say, 'Allah.'" [surah Luqman (31): 25] "Say, Who is (the) Lord of the seven heavens, and (the) Lord of the Great Throne?' They will say, 'Allah.'" [surah al-Mu'minoon (23): 86-87] Read the last verses of surah al-Mu'minoon, you will find that the disbelievers used to affirm tawhid ar-Rububiyah and similarly Allah Says in surah Yunus, "Say (to the disbelievers), 'Who provides for you from the sky and from the earth? Or who owns your hearing and sight? And Who brings out the living from the dead and brings out the dead from the living? And who disposes the affairs? They will say, 'Allah.'" [surah Yunus (10): 31] So the disbelievers affirmed tawhid ar-Rububiyah. tawhid is thus, NOT just the affirmation of tawhid ar-Rububiyah as the scholars of Kalaam (philosophers) claim. They claim that tawhid is the affirmation that Allah is the Creator, the Provider, the One who gives life and death. They say, 'He is One in His Self and there is no division to Him, He is One in His Attributes and there is nothing like Him, He is One in His Actions and there is no partner with Him.' - 31 - fact, only a part of tawhid, i.e.,) tawhid ar-Rububiyah (and not the complete tawhid). Refer to any book from the books of the scholars of Kalaam you will find that they do not go beyond tawhid ar-Rububiyah. tawhid ar-Rububiyah is not the tawhid with which Allah sent the Messengers, and affirmation of tawhid ar-Rububiyah alone does not benefit the individual because (of the fact that) the disbelievers and the leaders of disbelief affirmed tawhid arRububiyah, but it did not bring them out of disbelief and did not enter them into Islam. So, it is a great mistake (to define tawhid in the way of the scholars of Kalaam). Hence, whosoever believes this (i.e., tawhid is limited only to tawhid ar-Rububiyah), has not believed anything more than what Abu Jahl and Abu Lahab believed in. Today some educators stop at the affirmation of tawhid arRububiyah, and do not even touch (the subject of) tawhid alUluhiyyah. This is a great wrong (and injustice) with regards to the issue of tawhid. They also (wrongly) define Shirk in these words, "Shirk is that you believe that someone creates with Allah or provides alongside Him." We say (in response to them), 'This is the saying of Abu Jahl and Abu Lahab. They did not believe that someone creates with Allah or provides with Allah. Rather they affirmed that Allah is the Creator, the Provider, the One who gives life and death.' - 32 - Shaikh Muhammad ibn Abdul Wahhab writes, S ECOND
content_id:
Orang-orang kafir yang diperangi oleh Rasulullah ﷺ mengakui tauhid rububiyyah (tauhid ketuhanan Allah) — bahwa Allah adalah Pencipta dan Pengatur segala urusan — namun pengakuan ini tidak memasukkan mereka ke dalam Islam. Buktinya adalah firman Allah: "Katakanlah: 'Siapakah yang memberi rezeki kepadamu dari langit dan bumi? Atau siapakah yang kuasa (menciptakan) pendengaran dan penglihatan? Dan siapakah yang mengeluarkan yang hidup dari yang mati dan mengeluarkan yang mati dari yang hidup? Dan siapakah yang mengatur segala urusan?' Maka mereka akan menjawab: 'Allah.'" (QS. Yunus: 31)

Ini adalah Landasan Pertama: Mengakui rububiyyah Allah saja tidak cukup untuk menjadi seorang Muslim. Yang membedakan Muslim dari musyrik adalah tauhid uluhiyyah — mengkhususkan semua ibadah hanya kepada Allah, tidak kepada siapa pun selain-Nya.

Pelajaran penting: Banyak orang di zaman ini yang mengakui bahwa Allah adalah Pencipta, namun kemudian berdoa kepada wali-wali, meminta syafaat kepada orang yang sudah meninggal, dan melakukan berbagai bentuk ibadah kepada selain Allah. Mereka mengira bahwa mengakui Allah sebagai Pencipta sudah cukup — persis seperti argumen kaum musyrikin Arab yang diperangi oleh Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Landasan pertama ini membantah argumen tersebut dengan tegas.
